subject: EDO
preamble: This examination syllabus is drawn entirely from the senior secondary school curriculum for Edo which was developed by NERDC in 2006.
aims_and_objectives: enable candidates speak, read and write competently in Edo to communicate effectively with other speakers of the language;
familiarize candidates with the sound system, grammatical structures and other features of Edo Language;
acquaint candidates with the necessary tools needed for a thorough appreciation of Edo Speech Usage, Literature and Culture (customs and institutions).

scheme_of_examination.summary: The examination will consist of two papers, Papers 1 and 2. It will test all aspects of Edo Language: comprehension, composition, sound system, grammar and translation. It will also test selected texts of oral and written literature as well as Basic Principles of Literary Appreciation, Customs and Institutions. The rubrics shall be written in Edo.

papers.title: Multiple-choice
papers.details: Total of 60 items testing Language (30 items), Literature (22 items), and Culture (8 items).

papers.title: Essay-Type
papers.details: Candidates will be required to answer six questions in all: three questions from Section A (Language); two questions from Section B (Literature); and one question from Section C (Culture) using official Edo orthography and metalanguage.

topics.title: Composition
details: Types: Narrative, Descriptive, Argumentative, Expository, Dialogue, Debate, Letter writing.

topics.title: Sound System
details: Vowels, Consonants and Syllabic nasal (production in terms of place and manner, basic phonetic and phonemic classification).
Sound processes (e.g. vowel harmony, assimilation, elision).

topics.title: Grammar
details: Word classes: nouns, verbs, adverbs, pronouns, adjectives, etc.
Grammatical categories: subject, object, complement, predicate, number, person, tense, aspect, mood, etc.
The structure and type of sentences.

topics.title: Basic Principles of Literary Appreciation
details: Concepts and elements of literary appreciation in Edo literature.

topics.title: Oral Literature
details: Prose, Poetry, and Drama set texts in Edo oral tradition.

topics.title: Written Literature
details: Prose, Poetry, and Drama set texts in written Edo literature.

topics.title: Customs
details: Home training: greetings, respect for superiors, cleanliness, tolerance, integrity, hard work.

topics.title: Institutions
details: Politics: family structure, age grades, chieftaincy, Obaship.
Edo beliefs about Osalobua/Osanobua (God), deities/divinities, other invisible forces.
Economy: adverts, money lending, cooperative efforts.

appendices.title: SELECTED TEXTS (2014-2020)
details: Prose: 'Iden Nokpokhuo' by Osaren Omoregie; 'Oro' (pp 1-49) by Emovon & Osemwegie.
Poetry: 'Asikoko Ekharha Oghe Edo' edited by Obazee Gabriel et al.
Oral Literature: 'Izelogha' by Osemwegie, Osayomwanbo E.
Language & Culture Reference: 'Edo course Bk 4-6' by Dr. E. O. Imasuen; 'Edo phonetics and phonology' by Victor Edosa Omozuwa; 'Edo Proverbs and Figure of Speech' by Ogie Emwinma.

reading_list: Eghareuba, J. U. - A short History of Bini
